findings.detail: Read docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-05-17T05-05-44Z-atlas.json — gi=0.82 mode=green source=kv; 13 anomaly labels (elevated/critical); seed_result=200 OK; sustain_eligible=true; core kv_keys present.

GET /api/signals/micro 200 — composite≈0.673 gi≈0.673 with 9 instrument errors; each ATLAS-flagged label still present with watch/elevated severities (severities softened vs ATLAS critical labels but failures persist). Micro payload cycle C-306 vs integrity/quorum C-314 (metadata drift).

GET /api/integrity-status 200 — source kv (not mock); global_integrity 0.74 mode yellow degraded true terminal_status stressed; gi_verified false. Diverges from ATLAS catalog snapshot gi 0.82 and from live micro composite≈0.673.
GET /api/epicon/candidates 200 — count 0; no candidates, verification skipped (no POST /api/zeus/verify or /api/epicon/publish).
GET /api/quorum/state 200 — quorum_state substrate_pending (supplemental; C-298 POST /api/vault/attest still unavailable).
POST /api/vault/attest returned HTTP 404 (HTML not-found); quorum_status not returned — route not present on deployed surface.
POST /api/agents/journal returned HTTP 502 with github_403 — substrate canon write blocked; journal entry not registered.

recommendation: Reconcile ATLAS catalog heartbeat (gi 0.82 green) with KV integrity-status (0.74 yellow) and fresh micro composite before operator decisions; treat external instrument failures as persistent. Deploy or alias POST /api/vault/attest for C-298 quorum registration. Restore GitHub credentials for agent journal writes. Reconcile micro sweep cycle field with CURRENT_CYCLE.
